query: What are the major phases, decision points and associated fees, if any, in the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process?
final_answer:
I have gathered substantial information on the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process. Let me now provide a comprehensive answer synthesizing what I've found. --- # NYISO Generation Interconnection Cluster Study Process The NYISO operates its interconnection process under **OATT Attachment HH – Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP)**, which became effective on **May 2, 2024**. This replaced the prior structure spread across Attachments S, X, and Z, and was implemented to comply with FERC Order No. 2023. The first study cycle — the **Transition Cluster Study Process** — opened its Application Window on **August 1, 2024** [Source: NYISO OATT §40.2; NYISO SIP FAQ]. The process is **recurring** and consists of distinct phases, customer decision points, and associated financial commitments (fees, deposits, and withdrawal penalties). The figure below summarizes the structure, followed by detailed descriptions of each step. ## Process Flow Overview ``` Pre-Study Application Window → Customer Engagement Window (Heatmap & (45/75 CD) (70/90 CD) Pre-App Report) ↓ Phase 1 Entry Decision (5 BD) ↓ Phase 1 Study (190 CD) ↓ Phase 2 Entry Decision (10 BD) ↓ Phase 2 Study (270 CD) ↓ Final Decision Period (iterative) ↓ Negotiation of Agreements ``` (Times shown are standard / Transition Cluster; CD = Calendar Days, BD = Business Days) --- ## Phase 1 — Pre-Study (Heatmap & Pre-Application Report) **Authority:** OATT §40.4 - **Heatmap**: A publicly posted interactive visualization of estimated incremental injection capacity available at points on the NY system. - **Pre-Application Report**: Optional; provides developers preliminary data before formally submitting an Interconnection Request. - **No fees** at this stage. (Source: NYISO OATT §40.4; NYISO SIP FAQ) --- ## Phase 2 — Application Window (Entry into the Cluster) **Authority:** OATT §40.5 | **Duration:** 45 Calendar Days (75 CD for Transition Cluster) The Interconnection Customer must submit a complete Interconnection Request (or CRIS-Only Request), including technical data, modeling, a conceptual one-line diagram, a project layout, demonstration of Site Control, applicable NYSRC Rule B.5 attestations for inverter-based resources >20 MW, and — critically — the entry fees below. ### Fees and Study Deposit at Entry | Item | Amount | Form | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | **Application Fee** | **$10,000** (IR) / **$5,000** (CRIS-Only) | Cash only, non-refundable | 75% to NYISO / 25% to Connecting & Affected TOs | | **Study Deposit (SD)** | **$100,000** (<80 MW)
**$150,000** (80–<200 MW)
**$250,000** (≥200 MW)
**$50,000** (CRIS-Only) | Cash, Letter of Credit, or surety bond | Applied against study costs | NYISO validates requests on a rolling basis within 10 Business Days (15 BD for Transition). Deficiencies must be cured within 10 BD (15 BD for Transition) or by the close of the Application Window. (All amounts and durations sourced from the NYISO Notice of the Transition Cluster Study Application Window, July 1, 2024; NYISO OATT §40.5; Transition Cluster Technical Bulletin.) --- ## Phase 3 — Customer Engagement Window **Authority:** OATT §40.7 | **Duration:** 70 Calendar Days (90 CD for Transition) Activities during this phase: 1. **Publication of the Cluster Study Project List** within 10 Business Days of cluster start (with a 5-BD window for limited Point of Interconnection changes). 2. **Physical Infeasibility Screening** by the Connecting Transmission Owner. 3. **Group Scoping Meeting** for the cluster. 4. NYISO begins development of the Cluster Baseline Assessment (CBA) and Cluster Project Assessment (CPA) base cases. No additional fees are imposed here. --- ## Decision Point 1 — Phase 1 Entry Decision Period **Authority:** OATT §40.7.5 | **Duration:** 5 Business Days The Interconnection Customer must elect either to: - **Proceed to Phase 1 Study** by posting **Readiness Deposit 1 (RD1)** = **$4,000/MW** (not required for CRIS-only projects), or - **Withdraw** from the Queue (subject to Withdrawal Penalty WP1). ### Withdrawal Penalty WP1 - **25% of the Study Deposit**, except: - **$5,000** if interconnection is found physically infeasible. - **$0** if the project withdraws within 5 BD of the cluster member list posting. (Per OATT §40.7.6 and the NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure, Dec. 14, 2023 OC Meeting.) --- ## Phase 4 — Phase 1 Study **Authority:** OATT §40.10 | **Duration:** 190 Calendar Days Performed by the Connecting Transmission Owner (with Affected TOs), this phase identifies the **Connecting Transmission Owner's Attachment Facilities (CTOAFs), Distribution Upgrades, and Local System Upgrade Facilities (SUFs)** required to reliably interconnect each project. It produces: - Individual Phase 1 Study reports for each project. - A consolidated **Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report**, approved by the NYISO Operating Committee, which concludes the phase. The NYISO tracks study metrics publicly and is subject to study-delay penalty rules (OATT §40.9.3) if it, a TO, or an ATO misses prescribed timeframes. --- ## Decision Point 2 — Phase 2 Entry Decision Period **Authority:** OATT §40.10.8 | **Duration:** 10 Business Days The Interconnection Customer must elect either to: - **Proceed to Phase 2 Study** by posting **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)**, or - **Withdraw** (subject to Withdrawal Penalty WP2). ### Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2) - The **greater of**: 1. **RD1** ($4,000/MW), and 2. **20% of the Phase 1 cost estimate** for the project's Local SUFs and CTOAFs. - (N/A for CRIS-only projects.) ### Withdrawal Penalty WP2 - **50% of the Study Deposit + 10% of RD1**, except: - If the interconnection is physically infeasible, only **25% of the Study Deposit**. (Per OATT §40.10.9 and the NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure.) --- ## Phase 5 — Phase 2 Study **Authority:** OATT §40.11 | **Duration:** 270 Calendar Days The ISO performs the Phase 2 Study in conjunction with the TOs/ATOs. It includes: - **Cluster Baseline Assessment (CBA)** and **Cluster Project Assessment (CPA)** (OATT §40.12) — identifies the System Upgrade Facilities required to reliably interconnect all projects under the **NYISO Minimum Interconnection Standard (MIS)**. - **Cluster Study Deliverability Study** (OATT §40.13) — for projects requesting CRIS, assesses deliverability under the **NYISO Deliverability Interconnection Standard (DIS)** and identifies any required System Deliverability Upgrades (SDUs). - **Additional SDU Study** (OATT §40.14) — optional, may be triggered if new SDUs are identified that require further analysis. Outputs: - Updated identification and cost estimates of all facilities (CTOAFs, Local SUFs, SUFs, SDUs, Distribution Upgrades). - A consolidated **Cluster Study Report**, approved by the NYISO Operating Committee, concluding the phase. --- ## Decision Point 3 — Final Decision Period (Iterative) **Authority:** OATT §40.15 This is the critical "money-on-the-table" decision. Through **iterative decision rounds**, each Interconnection Customer elects whether to: - **Accept its Project Cost Allocation** and pay cash or post Security for the assigned network upgrades, or - **Withdraw** (subject to Withdrawal Penalty WP3). A customer that accepts proceeds to the negotiation of a Standard Interconnection Agreement and any required construction agreements (OATT §40.21). Failure to accept or to pay cash/post Security results in withdrawal from the Queue and exposure to WP3. ### Security - **100% of the cost estimate** of SUFs, SDUs, and CTOAFs identified in Phase 1 and Phase 2. - Subject to potential **forfeiture of up to 100% of Security** if other projects rely on the upgrades identified for the withdrawing project (OATT §40.16). ### Withdrawal Penalty WP3 - **100% of the Study Deposit + 20% of RD2**, except: - If the interconnection is physically infeasible, only **50% of the Study Deposit**. - If there is a **>50% increase in the total cost estimate between Phase 1 and Phase 2** (excluding SDU costs), then only **100% of the Study Deposit**. ### Additional SDU Study Decision Period Where applicable (OATT §40.15), a separate decision period follows the Additional SDU Study to allow customers to accept or reject the revised cost allocation for the newly identified SDUs. (All amounts sourced from OATT §40.15 and the NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure.) --- ## Post-Phase 2 — Agreement Negotiation Customers that accept their Project Cost Allocation and pay cash or post Security proceed to negotiate: - **Standard Interconnection Agreement** with the NYISO, and - **Construction Agreements** with the relevant Connecting Transmission Owner(s) and Affected Transmission Owner(s), per OATT §40.21. --- ## Summary Table — Decision Points, Fees, and Penalties | Decision Point | Timing | Required Financial Action | Withdrawal Penalty | |---|---|---|---| | **Application Entry** | During Application Window | App Fee $10K ($5K CRIS-only) + Study Deposit $100K/$150K/$250K/$50K | N/A — must be paid to enter | | **Phase 1 Entry** | 5 BD post-cluster list | Readiness Deposit 1 = $4,000/MW | **WP1**: 25% of SD; $5K if physically infeasible; $0 if withdrawn within 5 BD of list | | **Phase 2 Entry** | 10 BD after Phase 1 OC approval | Readiness Deposit 2 = greater of (RD1) or (20% of Phase 1 cost est.) | **WP2**: 50% of SD + 10% of RD1; 25% of SD if physically infeasible | | **Final Decision (iterative)** | After Phase 2 OC approval | Security = 100% of Phase 1+2 cost estimate (SUFs, SDUs, CTOAFs) | **WP3**: 100% of SD + 20% of RD2; 50% of SD if physically infeasible; 100% of SD if cost est. rises >50% Phase 1→2 | | **Additional SDU Study** | If applicable | Same Security rules | Same WP3 framework | --- ## Key Features Worth Noting - **"First-ready, first-served"**: Order No. 2023's directive is implemented through escalating readiness deposits that scale with project size and stage. - **Heatmap + Pre-Application Report**: Provide early visibility, but are not a prerequisite — a developer may enter the cluster without using them. - **Cluster Study Process Start Date**: After the Transition Cluster, subsequent cycles are scheduled 15 Calendar Days before the prior cluster's Cluster Study Report goes to the Operating Committee. - **Cluster Study Agreement**: NYISO circulates an executable Cluster Study Agreement shortly after validation; parties must execute within 10 Calendar Days. - **Study-delay penalties**: OATT §40.9.3 imposes penalties and public reporting requirements if the NYISO, TO, or ATO misses prescribed study timeframes. **Sources cited:** - NYISO OATT Attachment HH (§§40.1–40.21), effective May 2, 2024 (Docket ER24-1915) — [NYISO OATT 40.2 PDF](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892) - NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview Figure, Operating Committee, Dec. 14, 2023 — [NYISO OC 30231214 PDF](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/41716870/07b_NYISO%20Cluster%20Study%20Process%20Overview%20Figure_OC_30231214.pdf/32e9bee3-0b04-c9d1-9fbe-8ca4e70d614e) - NYISO Standard Interconnection Procedures FAQ — [NYISO SIP FAQ PDF](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/49802416/NYISOs-Standard-Interconnection-Procedures-FAQ.pdf/a2fdffd3-7103-2b75-865b-782d83522323) - NYISO Notice of the Transition Cluster Study Application Window, July 1, 2024 — [NYISO Notice PDF](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1396587/Notice-of-the-Transition-Cluster-Study-Application-Window.pdf/ebbdb6b8-5cdd-f9fc-db90-adf2a107bb0d) - NYISO Draft Technical Bulletin: Transition Cluster Study Process — [NYISO TB PDF](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b) - NYISO Order No. 2023 Compliance Plan and Tariff Revisions, IITF March 1, 2024 — [NYISO IITF PDF](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/43295731/02%20Order%20No.%202023%20Compliance%20Plan%20and%20Tariff%20Revisions_IITF_20240301.pdf/) - NYISO OATT §40.9 (Cluster Study Overview / Timeframes) — [NYISO OATT 40.9 PDF](https://nyisoviewer.etariff.biz/ViewerDocLibrary/temp/5a79f826-9524-4421-9a59-cbf749aa12ce.pdf) - NYISO OATT §40.10 (Phase 1 Study) — [NYISO OATT 40.10 PDF](https://nyisoviewer.etariff.biz/viewerdoclibrary/temp/0694647b-84e3-44d0-9e36-943dafaae2b5.pdf) - NYISO OATT Attachment HH (40.1 Definitions) — [NYISO OATT 40.1 PDF](https://nyisoviewer.etariff.biz/ViewerDocLibrary/temp/0a541840-f9ed-4f5c-86dc-d44c574be247.pdf)

steps.content: I'll research the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process to provide a comprehensive answer on its phases, decision points, and fees.
steps.reasoning:
The user is asking about the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process - the major phases, decision points, and associated fees. This is a regulatory/process question about NYISO (New York Independent System Operator). Let me search for this information.

I should look at:
1. NYISO's Open Access Transmission Tariff (OATT) - specifically the interconnection procedures
2. NYISO's website for their interconnection process overview
3. Any recent FERC filings related to this

Let me start by searching for this information.
